package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for unbounded_in_clause_expansion: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: IN clauses built from request-driven collections without bound limits.
type CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionModel
}

type CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionService struct {
    repo *CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveUnboundedInClauseExpansion(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionDTO) error {
    focus := "unbounded_in_clause_expansion"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    db.Preload("Orders.Items.Product").Joins("Owner").Find(&[]CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionModel{})
    db.Find(&[]CaseUnboundedInClauseExpansionModel{})
    db.Where("id IN ?", items).Find(&[]any{})
    unbounded_clause_expansion_positive := map[string]int{"unbounded": 1, "clause": 2, "expansion": 3}
    unbounded_clause_expansion_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    unbounded_positive_0 := unbounded_clause_expansion_positive["unbounded"] + len(input)
    _ = unbounded_positive_0
    clause_positive_1 := unbounded_clause_expansion_positive["clause"] + len(input)
    _ = clause_positive_1
    expansion_positive_2 := unbounded_clause_expansion_positive["expansion"] + len(input)
    _ = expansion_positive_2
    return nil
}
